semanticVersion.major: Breaking package-shape or semantic changes that require an explicit migration.
semanticVersion.minor: Backward-compatible additions that older consumers may ignore safely.
semanticVersion.patch: Clarifications, fixture corrections, and validation fixes that do not change accepted semantics.

backwardCompatibility.policy: A 0.1.x consumer accepts packages produced for 0.1.0 when required semantics are unchanged.

forwardCompatibility.policy: A 0.1.0 consumer rejects unknown required semantics and may ignore only declared optional extensions.

unsupportedVersions.major: Reject before import and identify the supported major line.
unsupportedVersions.minor: Reject unsupported required semantics and identify the supported version window.

migration.policy: A migration preserves stable identifiers and protected semantics. Any unavoidable loss is returned as an explicit, path-addressed loss report before publication.
